title: Exothermic Reaction Calorimeter with Adaptive Cooling
description: Closed-loop calorimeter monitoring an exothermic chemical reaction in a jacketed 500 mL vessel. DS18B20 array tracks spatial temperature at three heights; NTC thermistor measures jacket wall temperature. Cascaded PID drives SSR cooling pump to maintain vessel temperature within ±2°C of setpoint. Real-time enthalpy accumulation logged to serial CSV with over-temperature safety cutoff at 80°C.
objective: Measure instantaneous heat of reaction, maintain reactor temperature within ±2°C of setpoint, compute cumulative ΔH profile, and trigger emergency cooling halt on over-temperature fault.
constraints: Arduino Mega, 12V DC cooling pump, SSR-25DA, NTC 10 kΩ, DS18B20 ×3, max reaction vessel 500 mL, USB serial CSV logging, temperature cutoff at 80°C, 12V 3A supply.
design_element_titles: Cascaded PID Rate and Attitude Controller

element_usage_notes.DS18B20 1-Wire Temperature Probe Array: Three probes at vessel bottom, middle, and top to detect thermal gradients and compute mean reactor temperature.
element_usage_notes.Cascaded PID Rate and Attitude Controlle: Outer loop tracks vessel temperature setpoint; inner loop controls cooling pump PWM duty cycle via SSR.
element_usage_notes.SSR-Controlled Water Cooling Jacket: Actuates 12V pump based on PID output to circulate coolant through copper-coil jacket.
element_usage_notes.NTC Thermistor Calorimeter Jacket Sensor: Monitors jacket temperature to estimate instantaneous heat removal rate dQ/dt for enthalpy integration.

element_interactions.to: Cascaded PID Rate and Attitude Controller
element_interactions.description: Array average feeds outer PID loop as process variable for temperature control.

element_interactions.from: Cascaded PID Rate and Attitude Controller
element_interactions.description: PID output drives SSR PWM to modulate cooling pump speed and heat removal rate.

element_interactions.to: Cascaded PID Rate and Attitude Controller
element_interactions.description: Jacket temperature differential used to estimate heat flux for inner-loop rate control.
